era_context: The Old Kingdom of Egypt is under the Fourth Dynasty, a period of maximum centralized power and monumental construction. The Akkadian Empire is expanding in Mesopotamia under Sargon of Akkad (traditional dates c. 2334–2279 BCE, though disputed). The Indus Valley Civilization is in its mature phase. This era represents the height of Bronze Age civilization in the Old World.
disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: The year 2654 BCE is not documented in any surviving historical source. While the broader civilizational context (Egyptian Old Kingdom, Akkadian Empire, Harappan civilization) is well-attested through archaeology and later compilations, no specific events are recorded for this individual year. The historical record for this period is characterized by the absence of year-by-year documentation; only broader patterns and major reigns are known.
